vibe: Thursday unwind — deep house warmth, Tokyo city pop glow, lo-fi daydreams, and left-field discoveries from UK jazz to Japanese math rock

tracks.name: Sleeper
tracks.reason: You've been deep in the Osunlade/Kevin Yost/Moodymann zone lately — Jimpster lives right next door with that same warm, rolling deep house energy but a slightly UK-tinged flavour.

tracks.name: ノスタルジア・オブ・アイランド
tracks.reason: SPARKLE's already in your top tracks — this is the deeper PACIFIC cut, all sunset-over-the-ocean vibes. Perfect for Tokyo evenings.

tracks.name: 81Summer
tracks.reason: You know Nujabes well — Uyama Hiroto was his right hand on the production side. This solo joint is lush, jazzy lo-fi that hits different.

tracks.name: Sci-Fi
tracks.reason: Tainy doing reggaeton through a sci-fi electronic lens — given the Gorillaz and Damascus vibes in your rotation, this futuristic production style should click.

tracks.name: Mystery Lady
tracks.reason: Masego's 'TrapHouseJazz' thing bridges your R&B side (Sade, The-Dream) with jazz — silky saxophone over smooth beats. Proper chill.

tracks.name: Glue
tracks.reason: If Boy Harsher and Gesaffelstein are your dark electronic lane, BICEP is the euphoric side of that coin — nostalgic breakbeat energy that just builds and builds.

tracks.name: God Gave Me Feet For Dancing (feat. Yazmin Lacey)
tracks.reason: UK jazz at its most infectious — Ezra Collective won the Mercury Prize and this track with Yazmin Lacey is pure feel-good energy. Think Nujabes meets a London jazz club.

tracks.name: after image
tracks.reason: Japanese math rock legends — intricate guitar work that's almost meditative. You're in Tokyo, this is the city's sound distilled into shimmering post-rock.

tracks.name: You Are In My System - Club Mix
tracks.reason: Essential deep house from one of the genre's architects. If Moodymann and See You Again by &ME are in your heavy rotation, Kerri Chandler is non-negotiable.

tracks.name: Mic Check
tracks.reason: Cornelius is the Shibuya-kei godfather — this is hyperactive, sample-heavy Japanese electronic pop that feels like Gorillaz and math rock had a baby in a Tokyo record shop.
